feedback.text: I would like for the Act to include consumer protection for the video game industry permanently killing off video games and leaving consumers with a game they paid for that is now unplayable. Since it is already targeting nasty ways that the video game industry is monetizing (gambling-like features etc) I think this protection also fits right in. Today video game developers can remove their games from their online platforms when they see fit and without giving you a refund. There are plenty of reasons of why games may no longer be made available to the consumer who have bought and paid for the game. These include the developer implementing DRM (Digital Rights Management) features where even an otherwise offline single-player game stops working when the DRM server is no longer online, the developer releasing a sequel and wanting the consumers to go buy it and thus they pull their game off their platforms and make them unavailable even to people who bought and own the game, and of course the situations where the game itself is an online game and was never playable offline to begin with. There are no good reasons for why developers in the future should not design their new games with an End of Life plan in mind. Some already do and many more used to do this in the past. I can still play my old Nintendo and PlayStation games from my childhood, I just need to put the cartridge or disc into the video game console and connect it to a TV like I did back then. They still work. Future generations should have the same right and ability to do this. We are also losing a part of art and history when games are killed off like this and made unavailable for all time. I find it utterly unacceptable and highly anti-consumer. Games becoming digital-only and then being killed off only benefit these large multi-billion euro corporations and hurts the consumers and our culture at large. There is currently a European Citizen Initiative called "Stop Killing Games" that also aims to bring this to everyones attention and tackle this issue and it currently has over 1.4 million signatures. This is an issue that matters greatly to European consumers and also European culture. Video games are a massive industry and a huge part of our culture today. Please help protect our rights and our culture by no longer allowing game developers to permanently kill off the games we have bought and paid for. Not only should we have a right to play them whenever we want in the future as well but they are also such an important part of our culture and for many of us our lives. Like I mentioned previously I still have my old video games from when I was a child and it saddens me greatly to think that my future children might not have that opportunity due to corporate greed and consumer-hostile business practices.
